task: I’m working on a short school article and I have enough confirmed notes for it, but the draft feels a little plain. Can you help me make it read well without adding anything I can’t back up?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — paste your draft and your confirmed notes, and I’ll help you polish the writing while sticking strictly to what you can support.

I can help with:
- clearer wording and smoother flow
- stronger opening and transitions
- making it sound more polished or engaging
- keeping every claim grounded in your notes

If you want, I can also:
- keep the tone school-appropriate and simple
- make it shorter or more concise
- flag any places where the draft goes beyond your evidence

Send it over whenever you’re ready.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, here it is.

Draft: A burst water pipe in the school building caused a delay to first period on Tuesday morning. Students waited in the hallway while staff checked the damage and gave updates. The custodial staff worked to clean up the water, and some classes started later than usual.

Confirmed notes: 
- The pipe burst was near the first-floor hallway by the science rooms.
- First period was delayed by about 20 minutes.
- The district email said students should report to homeroom instead of their usual first-period classes.
- I talked to the custodian, who said the cleanup was already in progress by 8:10 a.m.
- A science teacher said her room was not damaged, but students were kept out of that hallway for a while.
- Two students said they were confused at first because they only heard people talking about it, not an announcement.

I just want it to sound less flat. Not dramatic, just cleaner.

turns.agent_response:
Here’s a cleaner version that stays within your notes:

A burst pipe near the first-floor hallway by the science rooms delayed first period on Tuesday morning. According to a district email, students were told to report to homeroom instead of their usual first-period classes. Students were kept out of that hallway for a while while staff checked the area and cleanup was already underway by 8:10 a.m. The delay lasted about 20 minutes. A science teacher said her room was not damaged, though students were still kept away from the hallway. Two students said they were confused at first because they only heard people talking about the problem, not an announcement.

If you want, I can also make it sound:
- a little more like a news article
- shorter and more direct
- simpler and more school-report style

user_simulator.text: Yeah, better. Still a little stiff though. Can you make it read more like a real school paper piece — same facts, just smoother, less list-y? Maybe with a stronger first sentence too.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a smoother version with a more natural school-paper feel, while keeping the same facts:

A burst pipe near the first-floor hallway by the science rooms delayed first period on Tuesday morning. Students were told in a district email to report to homeroom instead of their usual first-period classes, and the delay lasted about 20 minutes. While staff checked the area, students were kept out of that hallway and cleanup was already underway by 8:10 a.m. A science teacher said her room was not damaged, though access to the hallway was restricted for a while. Two students said they were confused at first because they only heard people talking about the issue, not an announcement.

If you want, I can also give you:
- a slightly more polished/newsier version
- a more student-written version
- a version with a stronger lead sentence but still simple

Can you make it sound a bit more natural and less stacked with details in the first two sentences? Maybe keep the lead strong, but not so formal. I’d want it to sound like an actual school paper story, not a template.
